Actions.use_case: Use to retrieve the properties of a disk.
List available VM sizes in a region for restore and export target selection.
List available compute SKUs in a region for restore and export target selection.
Read the runtime instance view of a VM (power state, OS provisioning state) for backup planning.
Discover protected VMs and read their configuration (disks, NICs, OS profile) for backup.
Read NIC IP configuration so the restored VM can be re-attached to the original subnet and address.
Discover the network interfaces attached to protected VMs.
Read network security group rules associated with protected VMs for restore planning.
Discover virtual networks available for restore and export targets.
Discover subnets available for restore and export targets.
Validate that a name (e.g. for a restore-target VM, disk, or storage account) is available before creation.
List Azure regions available in the customer's subscription.
List or get resource groups in the customer's subscription.
Enumerate resources within a resource group during discovery.
List or read blob container metadata in storage accounts referenced for backup.
Enumerate resources at the subscription level during discovery.
Read availability set membership of source VMs so restored VMs can be placed back into the correct availability set.
Returns the list of storage accounts or gets the properties for the specified storage account.
Read disk encryption set metadata to identify the customer-managed key used to encrypt protected disks.
Read shared gallery image version metadata for VMs that were provisioned from a gallery image.
Generate a time-limited SAS URI to read disk data when taking a snapshot.
Revoke the SAS URI generated for snapshot read access once the snapshot is complete.
Attach a restored VM's network interface to an existing network security group.
Place a restored VM into an existing subnet.
Attach a restored VM to an existing network interface.
Required for linking a disk access resource to a managed disk snapshot.
Read existing managed-disk snapshots created by Rubrik in the protection resource group.
Create a managed-disk snapshot from a protected VM's disk for backup.
Delete expired or failed snapshots from the Rubrik protection resource group.
Generate a time-limited SAS URI to read snapshot data so it can be transferred to the backup destination.
Revoke the SAS URI generated for snapshot data export once the transfer is complete.
Create the Rubrik protection resource group on first onboarding, or update tags.
Required to check subscription level locks during backup.
Apply a delete lock on the Rubrik protection resource group to prevent accidental removal.
Remove the Rubrik-managed lock on the protection resource group when offboarding the subscription.
Generate a service SAS for the Rubrik staging storage account so data-plane components can access blobs.
Create managed disks in the Rubrik protection resource group during restore or export operations.
Delete managed disks created in the Rubrik protection resource group during restore cleanup.
Delete network interfaces created in the Rubrik protection resource group during restore cleanup.
Create network interfaces in the Rubrik protection resource group for restored or temporary VMs.
Reference Key Vault secrets when deploying a VM that uses Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) keys.
Install or update VM extensions (such as the Azure Disk Encryption extension) on a restored or temporary VM.
Create or update temporary network security groups used during restore.
Delete temporary network security groups created during restore.
Create or update the Rubrik staging storage account in the protection resource group.
Create the blob container used to stage backup data and restore artifacts.
Delete the staging blob container after backup or restore artifacts are no longer needed.

DataActions.use_case: Read backup data blobs from the Rubrik staging container.
Write backup data blobs into the Rubrik staging container.
Delete backup data blobs from the Rubrik staging container after they are no longer needed.
